content: By 2025, retrieval-augmented memory agent architectures can be repurposed to provide grounded, experience-verified memory anchors for self-supervised world models, solving the common problem of unconstrained world model hallucination by only retrieving world modeling priors that the agent has actually encountered and encoded in long-term memory. This creates a hybrid 'grounded curious agent' that explores new environments via curiosity-driven active learning while never drifting into completely implausible world state simulations.
